definitions.text: Former ISO 639-1 language code for Indonesian.
Used to indicate location, inclusion, or position within spatial, temporal or abstract limits. Contained by; inside.
examples: The dog is in the kennel.
There were three pickles in a jar.
The dog is in the kennel.
There were three pickles in a jar.

definitions.text: Within the bounds or limits of.
examples: I like living in the city.
There are lots of trees in the park.

definitions.text: At or towards the interior of a defined space, such as a building or room.
examples: Suddenly a strange man walked in.
Would you like that to take away or eat in?

definitions.text: Towards the speaker or other reference point.
examples: They flew in from London last night.
For six hours the tide flows in, then for another six hours it flows out.

definitions.text: So as to be enclosed or surrounded by something.
examples: Bring the water to the boil and drop the vegetables in.

definitions.text: A position of power or influence, or a way to get it.
examples: His parents got him an in with the company.

definitions.text: One who, or that which, is in; especially, one who is in office.
The state of a batter/batsman who is currently batting; see innings.
Located indoors, especially at home or at one's office or place of work.

examples: Little by little I pushed the snake into the basket, until finally all of it was in.
The bullet is about five centimetres in.

definitions.text: Falling or remaining within the bounds of the playing area.
examples: If the tennis ball bounces on the line then it's in.
